title: SenML string length constants are not enforced by add APIs
description: API contract review finding. Public header lichen/subsys/lichen/senml/include/lichen/senml.h:43-47 defines SENML_MAX_NAME_LEN=32 and SENML_MAX_UNIT_LEN=8, and senml_encode_cbor() documents -EMSGSIZE for strings too long at lines 96-143. The add APIs store raw name/unit pointers without checking lengths in lichen/subsys/lichen/senml/senml.c:61-77, 85-101, and 108-123, while encode_record() later encodes base_name/name/unit with zcbor_tstr_put_term(..., 256) at senml.c:148-175. Impact: callers see public max constants but cannot rely on add-time validation; invalid names/units are accepted into a pack and may only fail later as a generic CBOR/buffer error, and strings between the advertised max and 255 bytes may be emitted successfully despite the published limits. This forces callers to duplicate length checks and makes SENML_MAX_* non-contractual. Fix direction: validate base_name/name/unit lengths when initializing/adding records and return -EMSGSIZE for violations, or update the header constants/docs to match the actual accepted limit and encode failure behavior.
